subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Taylor Polynomial, Euler's Method & Riemann Sum
stimulus:
The function $f$ is twice differentiable for $x > 0$ with $f(1) = 5$ and $f''(1) = 20$. Values of $f'$, the derivative of $f$, are given for selected values of $x$ in the table above.

| $x$ | 1.0 | 1.2 | 1.4 |
|---|---|---|---|
| $f'(x)$ | 8 | 12 | 18 |

parts.prompt: Write an equation for the line tangent to the graph of $f$ at $x = 1$. Use this line to approximate $f(1.4)$.

scoring_points.official_rubric: 1 point for tangent line. The tangent line at $x = 1$ uses the point $(1, f(1))$ and slope $f'(1)$.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct tangent line with slope $f'(1) = 8$ and point $(1, 5)$.
Uses the correct slope but wrong intercept; omits the point-slope adjustment to pass through $(1, 5)$.

required_elements: Substitute $x = 1.4$ into the tangent line equation

sample_responses.commentary: Substitutes $x = 1.4$ into the tangent line equation.
Forgets the $f(1) = 5$ offset; uses only slope times $x$.

parts.prompt: Use a midpoint Riemann sum with two subintervals of equal length and values from the table to approximate $\displaystyle\int_1^{1.4} f'(x)\, dx$. Use the approximation for $\displaystyle\int_1^{1.4} f'(x)\, dx$ to estimate the value of $f(1.4)$. Show the computations that lead to your answer.

scoring_points.description: Correct midpoint Riemann sum setup with subintervals $[1, 1.2]$ and $[1.2, 1.4]$, midpoints $x = 1.1$ and $x = 1.3$

required_elements: Two subintervals each of width $0.2$; midpoints at $x = 1.1$ and $x = 1.3$

scoring_points.official_rubric: 1 point for midpoint Riemann sum setup. Two equal subintervals of width $0.2$ with midpoints $1.1$ and $1.3$.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct midpoint setup — uses the midpoint of each subinterval as the sample point.
Uses the left endpoints of each subinterval — that would be a left Riemann sum, not a midpoint sum.

scoring_points.description: Correct midpoint values $f'(1.1)$ and $f'(1.3)$ read or interpolated from table, and correct sum
common_traps: Using the endpoints of the subintervals instead of the midpoints
Using $f'(1.2)$ and $f'(1.4)$ (right endpoints) rather than the midpoints $f'(1.1)$ and $f'(1.3)$
scoring_points.official_rubric: 1 point for Fundamental Theorem of Calculus / correct sum evaluation using table values.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct midpoint values consistent with the tabulated data and sum computed correctly.
Uses the wrong subinterval width; each subinterval has width $0.2$, not $0.4$.

scoring_points.description: Correct estimate of $f(1.4)$ using FTC and the Riemann sum approximation
scoring_points.official_rubric: 1 point for answer. $f(1.4) = f(1) + \int_1^{1.4} f'(x)\, dx$.

sample_responses.commentary: Applies FTC correctly to combine the initial value with the Riemann sum approximation.
Uses the Riemann sum value as $f(1.4)$ directly, omitting the $f(1) = 5$ initial value.

parts.prompt: Use Euler's method, starting at $x = 1$ with two steps of equal size, to approximate $f(1.4)$. Show the computations that lead to your answer.

common_traps: Using step size $h = 0.4$ (one step) instead of $h = 0.2$ (two equal steps)
scoring_points.official_rubric: 1 point for Euler's method with two steps. Uses $f(1.2) \approx f(1) + h \cdot f'(1)$ with $h = 0.2$.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct first Euler step using step size $h = 0.2$ and slope $f'(1) = 8$.
Uses step size $0.4$ for the full interval; the problem requires two equal steps of size $0.2$.

scoring_points.official_rubric: 1 point for the final Euler approximation.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct second Euler step using $f'(1.2) = 12$ from the table.
Uses $f'(1.4) = 18$ instead of $f'(1.2) = 12$; Euler's method uses the slope at the current $x$-value.

parts.prompt: Write the second-degree Taylor polynomial for $f$ about $x = 1$. Use the Taylor polynomial to approximate $f(1.4)$.

scoring_points.description: Correct second-degree Taylor polynomial centered at $x = 1$
common_traps: Forgetting to divide $f''(1) = 20$ by $2!$, giving $20(x-1)^2$ instead of $10(x-1)^2$
scoring_points.official_rubric: 1 point for Taylor polynomial. $P_2(x) = f(1) + f'(1)(x-1) + \dfrac{f''(1)}{2!}(x-1)^2$.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct second-degree Taylor polynomial at $x = 1$, with $f''(1)/2! = 10$.
Forgets to divide $f''(1) = 20$ by $2!$; the quadratic coefficient should be $10$, not $20$.
Correct substitution and evaluation of the Taylor polynomial at $x = 1.4$.
Forgets to square $(x - 1) = 0.4$ in the quadratic term.
